title: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore：关掉笔记本，让 AI 代理替你写代码
original_title: It’s safe to close your laptop now: Hosting coding agents on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime gives each agent session its own isolated microVM with a persistent workspace, secure tool access through Gateway, and built-in observability—so you can run Claude Code, Codex, Kiro, and Cursor in parallel without sharing secrets, ports, or filesystems. Close the lid, go to dinner, and pick up where you left off tomorrow.
source_category_title: AI 工具与开发者平台
topic_direction_description: AI 模型、Agent、开发工具、API、云服务和底层能力变化。重点看这次变化到底是什么、时间线如何、会不会改变普通技术人的工作方式、成本结构和工具链。

fact_summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore 是一个用于构建、部署和运行AI代理的平台。
AgentCore Runtime 为每个用户会话提供独立的微VM，实现CPU、内存和文件系统的隔离。
支持通过MCP服务器与Kiro、Cursor、Claude Code等编码代理集成。
提供持久化工作区功能（预览），可通过filesystemConfigurations配置。
有安全最佳实践文档，涵盖会话隔离、IAM、网络加密等。
有关于最小化启动延迟的官方文章。
定价页面存在但未抓取到具体价格，第三方博客称有12个组件计费。

Core services in Amazon Bedrock AgentCore What can you build with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore? Pricing for Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Next Steps Overview What is Amazon Bedrock AgentCore? Amazon Bedrock AgentCore is an agentic platform for building, deploying, and operating highly effective agents securely at scale using any framework and foundation model. With AgentCore, you can enable agents to take actions across tools and data with the right permissions and governance, run agents securely at scale, and monitor agent performance and quality in production - all without any infrastructure management. AgentCore services work together or independently with any open-source framework such as CrewAI, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, and Strands Agents and with any foundation model, so you don’t have to choose between open-source flexibility and enterprise-grade security and reliability. research_evidence.fetched_text: [Update] Session Storage has been added to Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime | DevelopersIO document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-theme', localStorage.getItem('theme')); developersIO produced by Classmethod Original English Contents [Update] Session Storage has been added to Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Strands Agents Amazon Bedrock 神野 雄大 2026.03.25 This page has been translated by machine translation. View original Introduction Hello, I'm Jinno from the consulting department who likes the Supermarket Life. On March 24, 2026, the Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Control Plane API was updated, adding the filesystemConfigurations parameter to configure Session Storage in AgentCore Runtime. https://awsapichanges.com/archive/changes/cfa448-bedrock-agentcore-control.html Previously in AgentCore Runtime, the filesystem state would be completely lost when a session ended, but with this feature, files can now be preserved across session Stop/Resume. ! Session Storage is a Preview feature as of March 2026. Specifications may change in the future. In this article, I'll test whether files truly remain even when stopping and resuming a session! Overview of the API Changes Among the changes this time, I'll focus on these three APIs in this article: API Change CreateAgentRuntime Added filesystemConfigurations parameter UpdateAgentRuntime Added filesystemConfigurations parameter GetAgentRuntime Added filesystemConfigurations to the response The main focus is on filesystemConfigurations . This article will primarily test this feature. Previous Behavior Let me first clarify the behavior before this change. In AgentCore Runtime, each session runs on its dedicated microVM. This provides VM-level isolation where CPU, memory, and filesystem are isolated per session

material_pack.why_this_topic_matters: AWS推出隔离微VM的编码代理托管服务，可能改变开发工作流，但成本和安全风险需警惕。
fact_summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore 是一个用于构建、部署和运行AI代理的平台。
AgentCore Runtime 为每个用户会话提供独立的微VM，实现CPU、内存和文件系统的隔离。
支持通过MCP服务器与Kiro、Cursor、Claude Code等编码代理集成。
提供持久化工作区功能（预览），可通过filesystemConfigurations配置。
有安全最佳实践文档，涵盖会话隔离、IAM、网络加密等。
有关于最小化启动延迟的官方文章。
定价页面存在但未抓取到具体价格，第三方博客称有12个组件计费。
官方文档列出了服务配额，但未明确持久化存储限制。

Core services in Amazon Bedrock AgentCore What can you build with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore? Pricing for Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Next Steps Overview What is Amazon Bedrock AgentCore? Amazon Bedrock AgentCore is an agentic platform for building, deploying, and operating highly effective agents securely at scale using any framework and foundation model. With AgentCore, you can enable agents to take actions across tools and data with the right permissions and governance, run agents securely at scale, and monitor agent performance and quality in production - all without any infrastructure management. AgentCore services work together or independently with any open-source framework such as CrewAI, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, and Strands Agents and with any foundation model, so you don’t have to choose between open-source flexibility and enterprise-grade security and reliability. research_evidence.fetched_text: [Update] Session Storage has been added to Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime | DevelopersIO document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-theme', localStorage.getItem('theme')); developersIO produced by Classmethod Original English Contents [Update] Session Storage has been added to Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Strands Agents Amazon Bedrock 神野 雄大 2026.03.25 This page has been translated by machine translation. View original Introduction Hello, I'm Jinno from the consulting department who likes the Supermarket Life. On March 24, 2026, the Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Control Plane API was updated, adding the filesystemConfigurations parameter to configure Session Storage in AgentCore Runtime. https://awsapichanges.com/archive/changes/cfa448-bedrock-agentcore-control.html Previously in AgentCore Runtime, the filesystem state would be completely lost when a session ended, but with this feature, files can now be preserved across session Stop/Resume. ! Session Storage is a Preview feature as of March 2026. Specifications may change in the future. In this article, I'll test whether files truly remain even when stopping and resuming a session! Overview of the API Changes Among the changes this time, I'll focus on these three APIs in this article: API Change CreateAgentRuntime Added filesystemConfigurations parameter UpdateAgentRuntime Added filesystemConfigurations parameter GetAgentRuntime Added filesystemConfigurations to the response The main focus is on filesystemConfigurations . This article will primarily test this feature. Previous Behavior Let me first clarify the behavior before this change. In AgentCore Runtime, each session runs on its dedicated microVM. This provides VM-level isolation where CPU, memory, and filesystem are isolated per session

for_gpt_editor.brief: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime gives each agent session its own isolated microVM with a persistent workspace, secure tool access through Gateway, and built-in observability—so you can run Claude Code, Codex, Kiro, and Cursor in parallel without sharing secrets, ports, or filesystems. Close the lid, go to dinner, and pick up where you left off tomorrow.

Core services in Amazon Bedrock AgentCore What can you build with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore? Pricing for Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Next Steps Overview What is Amazon Bedrock AgentCore? Amazon Bedrock AgentCore is an agentic platform for building, deploying, and operating highly effective agents securely at scale using any framework and foundation model. With AgentCore, you can enable agents to take actions across tools and data with the right permissions and governance, run agents securely at scale, and monitor agent performance and quality in production - all without any infrastructure management. AgentCore services work together or independently with any open-source framework such as CrewAI, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, and Strands Agents and with any foundation model, so you don’t have to choose between open-source flexibility and enterprise-grade security and reliability. research_evidence.fetched_text: [Update] Session Storage has been added to Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime | DevelopersIO document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-theme', localStorage.getItem('theme')); developersIO produced by Classmethod Original English Contents [Update] Session Storage has been added to Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Strands Agents Amazon Bedrock 神野 雄大 2026.03.25 This page has been translated by machine translation. View original Introduction Hello, I'm Jinno from the consulting department who likes the Supermarket Life. On March 24, 2026, the Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Control Plane API was updated, adding the filesystemConfigurations parameter to configure Session Storage in AgentCore Runtime. https://awsapichanges.com/archive/changes/cfa448-bedrock-agentcore-control.html Previously in AgentCore Runtime, the filesystem state would be completely lost when a session ended, but with this feature, files can now be preserved across session Stop/Resume. ! Session Storage is a Preview feature as of March 2026. Specifications may change in the future. In this article, I'll test whether files truly remain even when stopping and resuming a session! Overview of the API Changes Among the changes this time, I'll focus on these three APIs in this article: API Change CreateAgentRuntime Added filesystemConfigurations parameter UpdateAgentRuntime Added filesystemConfigurations parameter GetAgentRuntime Added filesystemConfigurations to the response The main focus is on filesystemConfigurations . This article will primarily test this feature. Previous Behavior Let me first clarify the behavior before this change. In AgentCore Runtime, each session runs on its dedicated microVM. This provides VM-level isolation where CPU, memory, and filesystem are isolated per session

支持通过MCP服务器与Kiro、Cursor、Claude Code等编码代理集成。
提供持久化工作区功能（预览），可通过filesystemConfigurations配置。
有安全最佳实践文档，涵盖会话隔离、IAM、网络加密等。

Core services in Amazon Bedrock AgentCore What can you build with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore? Pricing for Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Next Steps Overview What is Amazon Bedrock AgentCore? Amazon Bedrock AgentCore is an agentic platform for building, deploying, and operating highly effective agents securely at scale using any framework and foundation model. With AgentCore, you can enable agents to take actions across tools and data with the right permissions and governance, run agents securely at scale, and monitor agent performance and quality in production - all without any infrastructure management. AgentCore services work together or independently with any open-source framework such as CrewAI, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, and Strands Agents and with any foundation model, so you don’t have to choose between open-source flexibility and enterprise-grade security and reliability. research_evidence.fetched_text: [Update] Session Storage has been added to Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime | DevelopersIO document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-theme', localStorage.getItem('theme')); developersIO produced by Classmethod Original English Contents [Update] Session Storage has been added to Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Strands Agents Amazon Bedrock 神野 雄大 2026.03.25 This page has been translated by machine translation. View original Introduction Hello, I'm Jinno from the consulting department who likes the Supermarket Life. On March 24, 2026, the Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Control Plane API was updated, adding the filesystemConfigurations parameter to configure Session Storage in AgentCore Runtime. https://awsapichanges.com/archive/changes/cfa448-bedrock-agentcore-control.html Previously in AgentCore Runtime, the filesystem state would be completely lost when a session ended, but with this feature, files can now be preserved across session Stop/Resume. ! Session Storage is a Preview feature as of March 2026. Specifications may change in the future. In this article, I'll test whether files truly remain even when stopping and resuming a session! Overview of the API Changes Among the changes this time, I'll focus on these three APIs in this article: API Change CreateAgentRuntime Added filesystemConfigurations parameter UpdateAgentRuntime Added filesystemConfigurations parameter GetAgentRuntime Added filesystemConfigurations to the response The main focus is on filesystemConfigurations . This article will primarily test this feature. Previous Behavior Let me first clarify the behavior before this change. In AgentCore Runtime, each session runs on its dedicated microVM. This provides VM-level isolation where CPU, memory, and filesystem are isolated per session
